sections.paragraph:
ριγέω
Etym. ρῖγος
Etym. note ἐρρίγοντι for ἐρριγότι.

I. to shiver or shudder with cold: metaph. to shudder with fear or horror, Il., Soph.:—c. inf. to shudder to do, shrink from doing, Il.; also, ρ. μὴ . . , Od.

2. to cool or slacken in zeal, Pind.

3. to bristle with arms, Theocr.

II. trans. to shudder at anything, Il.
